task_completion.description: Verify that the file patient_profile.txt is created on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or terminal window showing a file named 'patient_profile.txt' located in /home/ga/Desktop/.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the required header and patient demographics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the exact header 'COMPREHENSIVE PATIENT PROFILE - VistA Data Completeness Report' along with patient identifier, date of birth, and sex.

task_completion.description: Verify that a specific patient is identified by full name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file clearly states the full name of the patient selected as having the highest composite score.

task_completion.description: Verify that the file contains data counts for at least 3 of the 4 clinical domains.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file lists numeric counts for at least three of the following sections: outpatient prescriptions, vital signs, allergies, and problem list entries.

task_completion.description: Verify that a numeric composite data completeness score is included.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file explicitly displays a calculated numeric composite score based on the domain counts.

task_completion.description: Verify that a clinical narrative of at least 3 sentences is included.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains a narrative paragraph consisting of 3 or more sentences discussing the patient's overall clinical complexity.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the VistA/CPRS system to gather patient data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the VistA/CPRS graphical user interface open and being used to browse patient records, rather than the agent just guessing or hallucinating data.

integrity.description: Verify that the clinical narrative is genuinely written and not just placeholder text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The narrative in the text file should read as a coherent clinical summary, not just a copy-paste of the prompt instructions or generic placeholder text.
